label: Warm Editorial
description: Broader content-first variant for publishing, CMS-led sites, and service storytelling.
signatureMotif: content-first rhythm, warm editorial hierarchy, and calm section flow
promptHints: Organize the experience around storytelling and reading cadence rather than conversion-only stacking.
Favor rich content hierarchy, callouts, and believable editorial structure.
styleRules: Keep the strongest layout rhythm from the reference, but adapt it to the chosen runtime scaffold.
Prefer polished, production-looking App Router structure over screenshot-faithful clones.
Favor editorial readability, spacing rhythm, and content hierarchy over card spam.
Prioritize content scanning, navigation clarity, and readable documentation rhythm.
avoidPatterns: Do not paste large reference code blocks verbatim into the generated site.
Do not let reference aesthetics override the user's actual topic, language, or route plan.
Avoid collapsing editorial references into generic feature-card marketing.
Avoid forcing documentation references into pricing-first SaaS structures.
worldClassRubric: Professional hierarchy above the fold: strong headline, CTA clarity, and balanced spacing.
Reusable sections with believable content, not generic lorem-style filler.
Accessible semantics and responsive layout decisions that survive a real Next.js preview.
Reading flow should feel deliberate on both desktop and mobile.
Content-first sites should feel structured, findable, and stable.
